investment_case: VWRA offers a broad all-world equity implementation that can cover both developed and emerging market exposure in a single sleeve candidate. The current case is less about making a tactical market call and more about using a diversified, low-turnover vehicle that keeps the sleeve role simple and explainable.
current_implication: The current implication is to keep VWRA near the front of the review set for global equity core exposure. The evidence supports continued comparison work, not immediate promotion language.
main_tradeoffs: Broad all-world coverage improves simplicity, but it can reduce flexibility versus splitting developed and emerging exposure.
A single-fund implementation lowers operational burden, but benchmark and regional mix differences still need bounded comparison language.
The diversified structure helps the core sleeve, but current evidence depth is not yet strong enough to overstate edge versus nearby alternatives.

baseline_comparisons.label: Against IWDA
baseline_comparisons.summary: VWRA offers a fuller one-ticket global allocation, while IWDA remains a simpler developed-markets baseline with different regional exposure.

baseline_comparisons.label: Against ACWI benchmark framing
baseline_comparisons.summary: The benchmark fit is directionally supportive, but product claims should stay bounded to implementation and sleeve-role language.

mandate_boundary: Do not turn the current review lead into an endorsement until benchmark support and replacement context are made explicit.
